title: rhus trilobata var. trilobata
intro: Rhus trilobata var. trilobata, also known as skunkbrush or skunkbrush acacia, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, derived from its bark, leaves, and roots. The health benefits of Rhus trilobata var. trilobata include treating skin irritations, wounds, and infections, as well as reducing pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to note that the plant's strong odor and potential skin irritation make it essential to use it in moderation and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
